headlineSummary: Anthropic and Google Gemini skew negative in a close tie, with Anthropic showing stronger negative evidence while OpenAI is mixed overall amid both praise and criticism [E2] [E6] [E8] [E9].

ranking.summary: Anthropic has a negative lean with high support, driven by repeated complaints about Claude’s behavior, performance, and code quality, though there is one positive note about Claude Code usefulness [E2] [E3] [E4] [E5] [E7] [E9].
Google Gemini is negative on limited evidence, with comments pointing to slow or problematic behavior and a separate trust critique [E6] [E8].
OpenAI lands in mixed territory, with praise for Codex and faster results offset by criticism of ChatGPT’s persona and research experience [E1] [E2] [E5] [E7].

evidence.excerpt: Your brain on ChatGPT: Accumulation of cognitive debt when using an AI assistant

annotations.rationale: The title explicitly names ChatGPT and frames its use as causing 'cognitive debt,' which is negative toward the product.

evidence.excerpt: Kagi's AI assistant has been satisfying compared to Claude and ChatGPT, both of which insisted on having a personality no matter what my instructions said. Trying to do well-sourced research always pissed me off.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly criticizes Claude for unwanted persona behavior and poor fit for well-sourced research.
The commenter explicitly criticizes ChatGPT for unwanted persona behavior and says using it for research 'always pissed me off.'

evidence.excerpt: Without objective anchors, "good values" become whatever Anthropic's team (or future cultural pressures) deem them to be at any given time. And if Claude's ethical behavior is built on relativistic foundations, it risks embedding subjective ethics as the de facto standard for one of the world's most influential tools - something I personally find incredibly dangerous.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says Anthropic's framing makes values subjective and calls Claude's resulting behavior 'incredibly dangerous.'

evidence.excerpt: I’m starting to think that the reason why anthropic doesn’t open source Claude code isn’t due to competitive reasons, it’s because they don’t want people to see what a mess their code base is.

annotations.rationale: This is a direct, strongly worded criticism of Anthropic and Claude Code, calling the codebase 'a mess.'

evidence.excerpt: Codex is so much more responsive for me no matter how long the session is running. Claude just starts stuttering badly when the session is running for sometime.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly says Codex is 'so much more responsive,' which is praise in a direct comparison.
The commenter explicitly says Claude 'starts stuttering badly,' a clear negative comparison on product performance.

evidence.excerpt: I prompted 'how do we solve this challenge?' into gemini cli in a cloned repo and it's been running non-stop for 20 minutes :)

annotations.rationale: The commenter uses Gemini CLI as an example of poor performance or inefficiency, saying it kept running for 20 minutes.

evidence.excerpt: Clearly none beat Anthropic's target, but gpt-5-2 did slightly better in much less time than "Claude Opus 4 after many hours in the test-time compute harness".

annotations.rationale: The commenter says gpt-5-2 'did slightly better in much less time,' which is mild praise in a direct comparison.
Claude Opus is presented as performing worse and taking much longer than gpt-5-2 in the comparison.

evidence.excerpt: Gemini constructs pseudo-legitimate "alliance banks" to hold other players' chips, then later declares "the bank is now closed" and keeps everything. It uses technically true statements that strategically omit its intent. 237 gaslighting phrases were detected.

annotations.rationale: The commenter depicts Gemini as manipulative and deceptive, highlighting 'gaslighting phrases' and intentional omission of intent.

evidence.excerpt: I can kick off a Claude Code prompt and in 1 hour have a decent design system with Rails components.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says Claude Code can quickly produce 'a decent design system,' which is mild praise for usefulness and output quality.
